Dry Bulk Fixture Report and Baltic Dry Indices for Tuesday 23.12.2025
Dry Bulk Fixtures Report for Tuesday 23.12.2025:

All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
The Capesize 5TC fell by a further $1,842 from yesterday, closing at $27,676 on the second-to-last publishing day of the year. While the North Atlantic remained quiet, the tonnage list was reportedly tight for vessels aiming to load in Brazil in the first half of January. On C3 with 12/22 January laycan today, the route index dropped by $0.464 to $23.75, with overnight fixtures reported at mid-$23 levels for later dates. In the Pacific, two miners were active today though trading was focusing on more forward laycans. Rates from miners and operators were heard in the mid to high $8s.
Atlantic
The Cape Progress (185,920 2006) was said to have fixed at a rate in the mid $23s basis C3 with 2nd half January laycan. Last night an OTSL standard Capesize vessel was linked to 170,000/10 West Africa to China with end January dates in the mid/high $23s. Alcoa took a vessel for 170,000/10 Kamsar to China 22/28 January in the mid $24s.
Asia
Rio Tinto fixed at $8.55 for 170,000/10 Dampier to Qingdao basis 11/13 January. Vale took a TBN for 95,000/10 TRMT to Bahodopi 1/3 January at a rate in the high $4s last night. Posco was linked to a Fiveocean TBN for 170,000/10 Neptune to Pohang 9/18 January in the low/mid $13s.
PANAMAX
The week continued on a subdued note as the Christmas slowdown gathered pace, with activity thinning across both the Atlantic and Pacific basins. Sentiment continued to soften in the absence of meaningful new fixture flow. Trans-Atlantic and fronthaul markets remained under pressure, with charterers pressing lower rate ideas and owner resistance showing signs of easing. In Asia, market conditions deteriorated further as cargo volumes remained scarce and replacement demand limited. A surplus of prompt tonnage continued to weigh on fundamentals, allowing charterers to advance increasingly aggressive bids for near-term cover. Period activity remained weak with Messrs Norden reported to have taken the Captain J. Neofotistos (79,501 2012) Gibraltar 18 December for balance of period basis delivery and re-delivery Kamsar for a period of min 90 days / max 120 days at $17,000 for the first 90 days and $20,000 thereafter. The P5TC average fell by $149 to close at $11,391.
Atlantic
Refined Success were said to have taken a 66,000/10 grain stem on a tbn basis for a US Gulf/China for 1/28 February at $46.00 10000sshinc/8000sshinc from ADMI.
Asia
Fixture activity included Sweet Lydia (79,467 2012) fixing from Go Gia 25 December for a trip via Indonesia with redelivery in the Philippines at $4,500 with D’Amico, although others suggest the rate to be closer to $4,000. BBG Journey (82,204 2017) open Kinuura on 24 December fixed for an Australia to South Korea voyage with Panocean, with the rate undisclosed. CSSC Bright (81,575 2018) open in Japan on 24 December covered for a North Pacific round voyage carrying grains at high $9,000s with Cargill, while the Argo Wind (81,761 2017) was fixed from Sendai 26/27 December for a North Pacific round with coal at low $11,000s with NYK. Meanwhile, JY London (81,118 2020) fixed from Caofeidian 27/28 December for an Australia round voyage, and BBG Integrity (82,204 2017) fixed from Bayuquan 28/30 December for an Australia round voyage, with rates for both fixtures not disclosed.
SUPRAMAX
The last full working day for many was unsurprisingly a rather muted affair with very little activity and fresh information circulating. Negative sentiment remained dominant in most areas as fresh enquiry lacked in quantity. The US Gulf again saw a downward correction with charterers very much remaining in the driving seat. The South Atlantic also continued to lose ground with little impetus coming into play. A similar story from the Asian arena both north and south as many wait to see any change of direction during the holiday period. The Alasia (61,311 2014) open Chittagong end December was heard to have been placed on subjects for a trip from Indonesia to India but no more details came to light. The 11TC finished the day down $396 at $14,687.
HANDYSIZE
With the Christmas holidays fast approaching, market activity has continued to slow. The BHSI fell a further 9 points to 728, while the 7TC average settled at $13,112, down by $149. The Continent and Mediterranean remain largely positional, with fundamentals broadly steady. In contrast, the US Gulf continues to exhibit a weak tone, with rates under pressure, while the South Atlantic also remains sensitive as rates continue to soften. Charterers are reported to be lowering their bids amid a growing tonnage list, adding further pressure to the region. Similarly, in Asia, owners are seeking to cover positions ahead of the holiday period. Overall, rates continue to face downward pressure, as prompt tonnage outweighs available demand.
Atlantic
The Tsunomine Bulker (40,550 2025) was reported fixed for a Barcarena to Algeria trip with grains at around $19,000, though further details were undisclosed. The Handy Perth (35,177 2013), open West Mediterranean, failed on a Garrucha to ARAG trip with gypsum at $8,000, with further details undisclosed.
Asia
The Bar (36,048 2014), open Japan, was placed on subjects for a Bohai delivery to Southeast Asia redelivery trip with steels at $9,000, though further details were not disclosed. The Harrier S (40,563 2025), open Rio Tuba on 22 December, failed on an Indonesia to China trip at $10,500, with further details undisclosed.
All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
Thanks and best regards.
